cards.title: Vulnerability Management Program Implementation
values: The simulated organization began without an established vulnerability management policy or operating process. The project needed to move from an unmanaged baseline to a repeatable program with governance, scanning permission, prioritized remediation, and verification.
Evidence chain

The full remediation cycle reduced total vulnerabilities from 32 to 4 across seven scans. Critical vulnerabilities were eliminated, high vulnerabilities decreased from 12 to 1, and medium vulnerabilities decreased from 17 to 2.

cards.title: DISA STIG Remediation Project
values: Selected Windows 11 DISA STIG findings required configuration changes and validation evidence showing failed, remediated, and passed states.
STIG evidence workflow
The project documents selected Windows 11 STIG remediations with script-based changes and validation evidence. The repository does not claim full STIG compliance or production deployment.

cards.title: Password Spray Threat Hunt: RDP Compromise Investigation
values: A cloud-hosted Windows VM in a cyber range showed activity consistent with password-spray-driven compromise over RDP. The hunt needed to identify the attacker source, compromised account, execution artifacts, persistence, evasion, discovery, collection, C2, and exfiltration attempt.
KQL investigation pivots

The investigation reconstructs a complete cyber-range attack chain and preserves reproducible KQL for major findings, including an attempted exfiltration path. It does not claim real-world containment or confirmed data theft.

cards.title: The Buyer / Akira Ransomware Threat Hunt
values: The investigation needed to determine how a ransomware event unfolded across AS-PC2 and AS-SRV, identify supporting evidence, map observed behavior to ATT&CK, and document detection gaps.
Ransomware Hunt Pivots

The report confirmed AS-PC2 and AS-SRV as affected hosts, documented a timeline from first known IOC on January 27, 2026 at 19:13:11 UTC through last known IOC on January 28, 2026 at 04:43:30 UTC, and identified detection recommendations for remote access misuse, Defender tampering, tool transfer, archive creation, shadow copy deletion, and privileged activity correlation.
